dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal requires detecting slope of prices (positive) and slope of momentum (negative) to identify bearish divergence. Used returns (positive = rising prices) and macd_hist (negative = falling momentum) as proxies for the required slope conditions. MACD histogram is the standard indicator for momentum divergence detection. Exit when divergence reverses (macd_hist crosses back above zero). Confidence increases with larger lookback period per original source.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal requires detecting slope of prices (positive) and slope of momentum (negative) to identify bearish divergence. Used returns (positive = rising prices) and macd_hist (negative = falling momentum) as proxies for the required slope conditions. MACD histogram is the standard indicator for momentum divergence detection. Exit when divergence reverses (macd_hist crosses back above zero). Confidence increases with larger lookback period per original source.

source_rule_text: When prices are rising and momentum is falling (bearish divergence), generate a sell signal.
